methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Thoron is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The valuation universe consists of seven private peers, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Airship Industries. No public comparables exist in this segment, limiting cross-market validation opportunities.

A peer-median valuation method is not applicable to Thoron at this stage. While four same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, none satisfy the minimum thresholds for meaningful statistical analysis—zero peers matched on same-stage status, zero on adjacent-stage proximity, and zero on similar valuation tier. The required minimum of three datapoints within any narrow cohort was not met. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial methodological noise and obscure rather than clarify valuation drivers specific to this company's risk and revenue profile.

Without sufficient peer clustering, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range through conventional comparables-based approaches. A defensible valuation would require alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis (contingent on revenue projections and cost assumptions), venture capital method analysis (dependent on exit scenario and return targets), or expansion of the comparable set through adjacent sectors with similar capital intensity and commercialization timelines. Key uncertainties include technology-to-market timing, regulatory pathways for autonomous deployment, and the breadth of addressable applications within the broader autonomous systems market.
